titleStmt.title: Letter from Jessie Crofton to Lady Clonbrock, 23 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Jessie Crofton

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Jessie Crofton (1865-1923) to her aunt Augusta Caroline Dillon, Lady Clonbrock (1840-1928). Clonbrock was a prominent member of the Irish Women's Association, founded to provide aid and assistance to Irish soldiers and prisoners of war and the wife of Luke Gerald Dillon, 4th Baron Clonbrock. Jessie's husband Arthur Edward Lowther Crofton (d. 1966) served as a lieutenant colonel in the Northumberland Fusiliers during the Great War. Jessie writes to inform Lady Clonbrock of the content of Arthur's last few letters. She describes the letters and gives an extract from one of the letters giving a detailed description of Arthur's experiences in the trenches.

msIdentifier.collection: Clonbrock Estate Papers, Collection List No. 54; Ms. 35,797 (2)

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
23_2_16. My dear Aunt Luce, Just a line to tell
you what was in Arthur's
last few letters. he came
out of the trenches on
Saturday after
They did not take their
clothes off the whole time
& Arthur was seedy
all the time. He has
He has a severe cough & can't
shake it off. He says
"there new trenches are
beastly the Germans blew
up two mines about
two months ago in front
of our line, with the result
that we both tried to get
the craters & are now
one on each side of it
only a few yards apart.
& its awfully lumpy earth
the Germans have the best
side & have good parapet
& we have nothing. then there
are dead men in front
who cannot be touched by either side & remains
of men in the parapet
etc. & the most awful
stench & you cannot show
yourself or you get hit
at once, & there is more
shelling here than in
the last trenches. I had
a better dugout & can
stand up straight &
Someone collected some chairs
from a farm hence that
was shelled before the
people could remove their
furniture — Edward has
been to see him & looks
well, & Arthur is expecting
him again. Arthur says
he heard from Captain White
who said Col Sapte was
on his way home, because
he is too old! he is a good
bit younger than Arthur.
Arthur likes his 2nd in
Command, so that is a good
thing — Arthur's Batl<hi rend="superscript><hi rend="underline>n is the
13th so I suppose young
is one of his officers. he said the
last two who came out
were rankers, but as far
as he could see they
were no worse than a
lot of the others. he
has some gentleman still
which is a good thing
with most of them going.
His 2nd in C. is alright
so that is a comfort, I
expect Arthur knows young
Haig by sight but I don't
think he knows him
by name. In any case
he would be nice &
civil to him — Markie
has a new Cap & Scarf
having been made a
Gym Officer —they played
Eton, at Eton on Saturday
& beat them 35 35 to 6.
& had a most enjoyable
day — they motored to
Eton at a tremendous
pace & turned all
the corners on one
wheel, so were quite
happy. Edward is so much
better since his nose was
done. I suppose that is
why he passed the
Medical Board this
time. I found applications
he had sent in at
various times (among his
papers) to be sent out
to the front, had been
returned to him, saying
he could not go until
he had passed a
Medical Board. I thought he was applying
but he would not say
when I asked him if
he was. I'm very glad
he has gone to his
own Regt. I am
Yours aff's
Jessie C
Feb:25
Jessie
